<result>WS-C cleanup + documentation complete. All tasks done, verified, committed, and pushed.

## Changes (file:line)

**Task 1 — dead `default` key: REMOVED (not commented).**
- `config/logger.php:31` — removed `'default' => 'file',`. Grep confirmed it was the only reference in the repo; `LoggerFactory::loadConfig()` / `StructuredLogger::setupHandlers()` never read `config['default']` (the other `['default']` hits in `src` are unrelated — subtitle/track/plugin-schema dispositions). Chose removal over a comment because the key implied routing that does not exist; app.log's firehose comes solely from the untagged `file` handler.
- `config/logger.php` top comment — added a NOTE block explaining there is deliberately no `default` key and why the catch-all actually works (untagged handler → all channels), so a future reader does not re-add it.

**Task 2 — accurate docblocks: verified, no code change needed.**
- `src/Common/Logger/StructuredLogger.php` docblocks for `setupHandlers()` / `handlerAppliesToChannel()` (lines 114-124) / `handlerEnvEnabled()` (lines 134-145) and the class-level routing note (lines 51-55) already accurately describe the real routing: untagged handler → all channels (app.log + error.log); `channels`-tagged → only those channels (plugins.log=plugins, events.log=events); `env`-tagged → only when the env var is truthy (events.log requires `PHLIX_DEBUG_EVENTS`, events still captured in app.log otherwise).

# WS-B — phlix-console-client: poster rails + detail/search/photo images (plan_posters.md §4)

Shipped commits: ba87f01 (rails topLevel=1), 1ad61f2 (DetailScreen resolveUrl),
5a9b346/3eedc04 (SearchScreen resolveUrl), 4461e28 (Photos resolve-before-scheme fix +
repaired resolveUrl tests), 9e73a97 (repaired stale Photos thumbnail tests).
Prior CHANGELOG entry af69668 covered only the initial poster fixes (pre-Photos, pre-test-repair).

## Scribe — 2026-07-18

- `/home/sites/phlix/phlix-console-client/CHANGELOG.md` — expanded the WS-B "Fixed" entry:
  added the Photo library (`PhotoAlbumScreen`/`PhotosScreen`) resolve-before-scheme-check fix,
  noted rails `topLevel=1` leaves series drill-in (parentId) listing episodes, and documented
  the genuine test-coverage repairs (real local HTTP server + deterministic scheduled-load
  counts for `resolveUrl()`; `MediaQueryTest` asserts `forLibrary()` emits `topLevel=1`).
  Committed 79e1a58 and pushed to master.
- Docblocks: verified already present and concise — no code edits needed. `MediaQuery::forLibrary()`
  (MediaQuery.php:41-45), `resolveUrl()` in DetailScreen.php:332-336, SearchScreen.php:328-331,
  PhotoAlbumScreen.php:237, PhotosScreen.php:289; resolve-before-scheme rationale comments already
  inline in fetchHero/loadCoversIn/loadChildPostersIn. No behavioral change, so phpstan not re-run.
- phlix-docs: NO console-client page exists (dev/ has client-mobile/roku/tizen/windows but no
  client-console; clients/overview.md does not list the TUI client; no sidebar slot). Per the
  "note clearly, don't invent a whole new page" rule, did not author a full new build-guide page
  for a poster-URL bug fix. phlix-docs was NOT modified — no commit there.
- phlix-console-client README.md: left unchanged — it is a user quickstart with no natural home
  for the internal relative-URL-resolution note.

tool_input.prompt:
STRICTLY READ-ONLY audit. Do NOT Edit/Write/commit/mutate anything. Verify by reading ACTUAL code (Smarty .tpl originals, Vue page source, route tables, server APIs) — not changelogs. Do NOT run `caliber refresh`. Always `cd /home/sites/phlix/<repo> && <cmd>` in the same command.

GOAL: Produce a precise IMPLEMENTATION SPEC for finishing WS-D-SRV (plan_posters.md §6, phlix-server Smarty→/app migration). Deep eval already established: the Vue pages for Books/Audiobooks/Photo/Search/Security/Music-subpages EXIST in phlix-ui source and their server APIs exist, BUT none are registered in any route table (unreachable), passkeys page isn't a Settings tab, and there's a phpstan error at WebPortalRouter.php:2884. Smarty deletion is DEFERRED (owner will verify first) — so do NOT spec any deletion. Your spec is ONLY: routing + nav + FEATURE-PARITY gaps + the phpstan fix.

For EACH surface below, produce: (A) the Smarty original's feature list (read the .tpl files under /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server/public/templates/{books,audiobooks,photo,music,auth,search} — enumerate every user-facing feature/action/field/control the Smarty page offers); (B) the current Vue page's feature list (read the .vue source in /home/sites/phlix/phlix-ui/src/pages/); (C) PARITY GAPS — features in Smarty missing from Vue (be specific: which button/field/action/state); (D) the exact ROUTE registration needed (path, component import, props) and WHERE to add it; (E) NAV entry needed and where.

SURFACES:
1. Books: Smarty books/{books,book,reader}.tpl → Vue BooksPage.vue, BookDetailPage.vue, BookReaderPage.vue (exported phlix-ui/src/index.ts ~285-287). Server API /api/v1/books* (Application.php ~1742-1752).
2. Audiobooks: Smarty audiobooks/{audiobooks,audiobook,player}.tpl → Vue AudiobooksPage/AudiobookDetailPage/AudiobookPlayerPage (index.ts ~288-290). API /api/v1/audiobooks* (~1795-1807).
3. Photo: Smarty photo/{albums,album,photo,slideshow}.tpl (+partials, EXIF) → Vue PhotoAlbumsPage/PhotoAlbumPage/PhotoViewPage/PhotoSlideshowPage (index.ts ~291-292) + src/api/photos.ts. API /api/v1/photo/* (~1826-1838). PAY SPECIAL ATTENTION to slideshow + EXIF metadata display parity.
4. Search: Vue SearchPage.vue (index.ts ~278) → /api/v1/media/search (searchMedia WebPortalRouter.php ~294/1504). (May have no Smarty original — note if so; still needs routing.)
5. Security/passkeys (WebAuthn): Smarty auth/webauthn-settings.tpl (/settings/security) → Vue SecuritySettingsPage.vue (index.ts ~279). API /api/v1/me/webauthn/credentials + /api/v1/auth/webauthn/register/* (Application.php ~683-688). Determine: should it be a route (/app/settings/security) AND/OR a tab inside SettingsPage.vue (which currently has only appearance/playback/server tabs)? Recommend the cleaner option and spec it.
6. Music sub-pages: Vue MusicArtistsPage/MusicArtistPage/MusicAlbumPage/MusicTracksPage/MusicPlayerPage (index.ts ~280-284). Only /music→MusicLibraryPage is currently routed (createPhlixApp.ts ~304). API getMusicTracks (WebPortalRouter.php ~2861). Spec routes for the sub-pages + how MusicLibraryPage should link to them.

ALSO SPEC:
- The ROUTE-REGISTRATION MECHANISM: read phlix-ui/src/app/createPhlixApp.ts buildRoutes() (~207-328), phlix-ui/src/app/admin.ts, and phlix-server/web-ui/src/main.ts extraRoutes (~18-29). Decide for EACH surface whether the route belongs in phlix-ui's shared buildRoutes() (so both hub+server get it) or ONLY in phlix-server/web-ui/src/main.ts extraRoutes (server-only). These are SERVER media surfaces → most likely server-only extraRoutes, but confirm against how /music and LibraryScanPage are wired. Give the EXACT code shape to add (import + route object) for each.
- The NAV/menu wiring: read phlix-server/web-ui/src/main.ts menu config (browse/settings/admin) — spec where to add nav entries for the media libraries (Books/Audiobooks/Photos/Music) so users can reach them. Check if there's a libraries-driven dynamic menu (the server exposes library types) vs a static menu.
- phpstan fix: read WebPortalRouter.php ~2861-2900 getMusicTracks; quote the exact offending line ~2884 and give the precise corrected expression (is_scalar guard) that clears the cast.string error.
- Are there existing vitest specs for any of these pages? (parity test gap.)

Run (read-only): 
- `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-ui && ls src/pages | grep -iE "book|audio|photo|music|search|security"; sed -n '200,330p' src/app/createPhlixApp.ts`
- `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && ls public/templates/books public/templates/audiobooks public/templates/photo public/templates/music public/templates/auth 2>/dev/null; sed -n '1,40p' web-ui/src/main.ts`
- `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && vendor/bin/phpstan analyse src/Server/WebPortal/WebPortalRouter.php 2>&1 | tail -15`

REPORT: a structured per-surface spec (A–E as above), the exact route-registration code shapes, the nav wiring plan, the phpstan fix, and the test gap. This spec will directly drive implementation agents, so be concrete (file paths, line anchors, exact import/route snippets, exact missing features). Report is DATA for the parent.

{"stdout":"## 6. WORKSTREAM D — Retire the legacy Smarty UI (migrate → then delete)

**Principle (owner's explicit instruction): migrate every Smarty-only surface into the `/app` UI (phlix-ui) — or
wherever appropriate — and verify it, BEFORE removing any Smarty. Never delete a surface that has no `/app`
equivalent yet.**

Both hubs redirect `/` → `/app`; phlix-ui admin coverage is **broader** than Smarty ever was. But several
end-user/owner surfaces still exist **only** in Smarty. WS-D is a two-phase program per repo:
**(1) migrate the gaps to `/app`, verify parity; (2) delete the dead Smarty stack.**

Because migration adds pages to **phlix-ui** (the shared package) and/or the per-app `web-ui` consumers, the
**UI rebuild/release runbook (Appendix A) is REQUIRED** in WS-D and must run after each phlix-ui change batch.

### 6.0 Where new pages live (decide per surface in the D-*-0 read step)

Determine, for each surface, whether the page belongs in the **shared `phlix-ui` package** (e.g. via
`buildHubAdminRoutes` / `buildAdminRoutes` / the app route tables) or in the thin **consumer `web-ui`**
(`phlix-hub/web-ui` or `phlix-server/web-ui`). Hub already routes hub pages through phlix-ui
(`src/app/admin.ts` `buildHubAdminRoutes`; `MyServersPage`, `ManageSharesPage`, `FederationPage`), so most new hub
pages go in phlix-ui. Confirm the pattern before implementing each.

### Phase D-HUB — phlix-hub Smarty retirement

**Smarty-only surfaces to migrate first (backing JSON APIs already exist — front-end-only work):**

| # | Surface | Smarty template / route | Existing API |
|---|---------|-------------------------|--------------|
| 1 | Media requests (user) | `/requests`, `home/requests.tpl` | `/api/v1/me/requests` |
| 2 | Admin request-approval queue | `/admin/requests`, `home/admin-requests.tpl` | `/api/v1/admin/requests` |
| 3 | Shared-with-me (incoming shares) | `/shared-with-me`, `home/shared-with-me.tpl` | `/api/v1/me/shares` (incoming) |

/**
 * Admin routes + menu seam (RA — admin port; H0 — composable page groups).

 * The shared admin surface is an **opt-in, per-consumer** section: a host app
 * spreads `buildAdminRoutes()` (server) or `buildHubAdminRoutes()` (hub) into its
 * `PhlixAppConfig.extraRoutes`. The pages nest under a single parent route that
 * renders {@link AdminLayout} (a sidebar of the admin links + a `<RouterView>`),
 * so the admin section gets its own navigation chrome without the host shell
 * having to render a nested menu. Every page — and the layout itself — is a
 * lazily-imported chunk, so a consumer that never shows admin doesn't pay for it.
 *
 * **Page groups (H0).** Admin pages are exported as three registries so each app
 * mounts only the surfaces it has a backend for:
 * - {@link commonAdminPages} — portable to BOTH apps: Users, Logs, Settings.
 * - {@link serverAdminPages} — media-server-only: Dashboard, Webhooks, Services,
 *   Integrations, Backup, Cast Devices, DLNA, Remote Access, Live TV, Collections,
 *   History, SyncPlay, Libraries, Duplicates, Plugins.
 * - {@link hubAdminPages} — hub-only: Hub Dashboard, Audit Logs.
 *
 * `buildAdminRoutes(base)` with no `pages` argument yields the **server pages in
 * their original sidebar order** (Dashboard first), so the server's admin landing
 * page and sidebar ordering are unchanged (U6 adds Plugins after Libraries).
 * `buildServerAdminRoutes` is the explicit synonym for that default;
 * `buildHubAdminRoutes` mounts the hub set (Hub Dashboard, Users, Logs, Settings,
 * Audit Logs). Each child keeps a stable `admin-*` route name and resolves to
 * `<base>/admin/<segment>`.
 */

- Smarty-only removable: `public/templates/**` (all .tpl), `public/assets/js/*.js` + `public/assets/css/app.css`, `src/Common/WebPortal/PageRenderer.php`, `src/Http/Controllers/PageController.php`, `smarty/smarty:^4.0` (composer.json:14), `var/smarty/{compile,cache}` (created on-demand by PageRenderer::newSmarty).

cat >> /home/sites/phlix/steps/hub-smarty-legacy-audit.worklog.md <<'EOF'

## general-purpose — WS-D-HUB finish spec (surface #7 fix + 6-page parity + tests) — 2026-07-18

READ-ONLY audit. Verified against actual .tpl originals, Vue source, route tables, InviteLinkController.

### Surface #7 (public invite acceptance) — BROKEN, spec below
- `GET /invite/{token}` (Application.php:1079-1081) redirects → `/app/invite/{token}` which has NO Vue route → SPA no-match/404. `InviteLinkController::showAcceptInvitePage` (renders accept-invite.tpl) is DEAD (no longer routed).
- Redeem: `POST /api/v1/me/invite-links/{token}/redeem` (InviteLinkController::redeem) is AUTH-GATED (401 auth.required if no user; 201 LibraryShare on success; 400 invalid_invite; 404 invite_link_not_found; 410 invite_expired_or_exhausted). No public redeem endpoint exists and none is needed.
- accept-invite.tpl behavior: unauth → login/signup CTAs (redirect back to /invite/{token}); auth → single "Accept Invite" button → POST redeem → on success redirect /app/shared-with-me; JS maps 401/410/404. NO invite metadata displayed (generic "You've been invited to access a library!").
- FIX PLAN: create `phlix-ui/src/pages/AcceptInvitePage.vue`, export in src/index.ts, register in hub `web-ui/src/main.ts` extraRoutes: `{ path:'/app/invite/:token', name:'accept-invite', component:AcceptInvitePage, props:true, meta:{ public:true } }`. meta.public:true is the guard opt-out (createPhlixApp.ts:71). Hub redirect (Application.php:1080) becomes CORRECT once page exists — no server change.
- BLOCKER for auth flow: `LoginForm.vue:54-55` always `router.push(config.home)` and IGNORES `route.query.redirect` (guard sets redirect at createPhlixApp.ts:77 but nothing consumes it). Must patch LoginForm.vue (+ SignupForm) to honor an internal `/app`-prefixed `route.query.redirect`. Otherwise login→return-to-invite loops back to /app/servers.

### Surfaces #1-6 parity (Vue vs Smarty) — all migrated, minor gaps only
| # | Page | Route | API | Parity |
|---|------|-------|-----|--------|

## general-purpose — WS-D-SRV routing/nav/parity/phpstan spec — 2026-07-18

READ-ONLY audit. No files mutated. Verified against actual Smarty .tpl + Vue .vue + route tables + server routes.

### Headline findings
- Every Vue page (Books/Audiobooks/Photo/Search/Security/Music sub-pages) is EXPORTED from
  `phlix-ui/src/index.ts` (lines 269, 278-292) and is a FEATURE SUPERSET of its Smarty original.
  The Vue pages are 250-854 lines vs 15-224-line Smarty templates. NO material feature-parity gaps found.
- NONE of these routes are registered anywhere (buildRoutes, admin.ts, or server web-ui main.ts).
  All server media surfaces are currently unreachable in `/app`.
- Route-registration mechanism = add imports + route objects to
  `phlix-server/web-ui/src/main.ts` `extraRoutes` (server-only, mirrors the existing LibraryScanPage
  precedent). `/music` is the lone exception already in shared `buildRoutes()`.
- Passkeys: recommend adding a 4th tab to `SettingsPage.vue` (matches Smarty `/settings/security`),
  not a standalone route.
- phpstan error WebPortalRouter.php:2884 = `(string)($row['id'] ?? '')` casting mixed. Fix with is_scalar guard.
- Zero vitest specs exist for any of these pages (MusicLibraryPage has one, but the sub-pages do not).

### Route names the Vue pages already hard-code (MUST match on registration)
- books → BooksPage; book-detail (:id) → BookDetailPage; book-reader (:id) → BookReaderPage
- audiobooks → AudiobooksPage; audiobook-detail (:id) → AudiobookDetailPage; audiobook-player (:id) → AudiobookPlayerPage
- Photo = PATH-based (no route names): /app/photo/albums, /app/photo/album/:id, /app/photo/photo/:id, /app/photo/slideshow
- music-artist (:name, props:true), music-album (:name, props:true); MusicTracksPage/MusicPlayerPage standalone
- Search = path /app/search?q=... (name 'search'); Security = SettingsPage tab.

### phpstan fix (WebPortalRouter.php:2884)
Offending: `'id' => is_string($row['id'] ?? null) ? $row['id'] : (string)($row['id'] ?? ''),`
Fix: extract `$rawId = $row['id'] ?? null;` then
`'id' => is_string($rawId) ? $rawId : (is_scalar($rawId) ? (string)$rawId : ''),`

Full spec delivered to coordinator in the agent final message.

<script setup lang="ts">
/**
 * AcceptInvitePage (WS-D-HUB #7) — the PUBLIC invite-acceptance surface.
 *
 * Reached at `/app/invite/:token`, where the hub redirects the public
 * `GET /invite/{token}` link. This is the only genuinely broken hub surface: the
 * PHP redirect had no matching Vue route (404). This restores parity with the
 * retired Smarty `home/accept-invite.tpl` + `accept-invite.js` flow:
 *
 *   - NOT logged in → generic "you've been invited" message + Log In / Sign Up
 *     buttons that carry a `?redirect=/app/invite/<token>` hop back here (honoured
 *     by LoginForm/SignupForm's safe-redirect guard) so the user returns to accept.
 *   - Logged in → an "Accept Invite" button → `POST /api/v1/me/invite-links/{token}/redeem`
 *     (auth-gated). On success a confirmation + a "View Shared Libraries" button
 *     to `/app/shared-with-me`. Errors map: 401 re-login, 404 not found, 410
 *     expired/exhausted, else generic.
 *
 * The route is `meta: { public: true }` so the auth guard lets an unauthenticated
 * visitor reach it. `isLoggedIn` here is only a token-PRESENCE flag (the public
 * route skips `auth.init()`); a stale token simply surfaces as a 401 on redeem,
 * which routes the user to log in again — matching the Smarty behaviour.
 *
 * `client` is an injectable test seam; it defaults to the shared `api` singleton.
 */

  /** The opaque invite token from the URL (may contain `/` — captured via `:token(.*)`). */

READ-ONLY review. Do NOT Edit/Write/commit/mutate anything. Do NOT run `caliber refresh`. Always `cd /home/sites/phlix/<repo> && <cmd>` in the same command.

Review WS-D-SRV server-side commit `37a76a43` in /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server. Diff: `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && git show 37a76a43`.

Context: The Books/Audiobooks/Photo/Search/Music-subpage Vue pages already exist in @phlix/ui but were unrouted (unreachable). This commit registers them in `web-ui/src/main.ts` extraRoutes + adds a static nav menu, and fixes a phpstan cast in `src/Server/WebPortal/WebPortalRouter.php` getMusicTracks.

Review for:
1. ROUTE CORRECTNESS: do the 16 route names/paths EXACTLY match what the page components hard-code (they use `router.push({name:...})` and path strings — a mismatch = dead navigation)? Verify a sample by checking the actual page sources if available in `phlix-server/web-ui/node_modules/@phlix/ui` (dist) or note they were verified. Are `music-artist`/`music-album` correctly `props:true` for their `:name` param? Any path collision with existing built-in routes (e.g. does `/app/search` or `/app/music/*` clash with shared buildRoutes)? Does `/app/photo/photo/:id` (odd doubled segment) match what PhotoViewPage pushes?
2. GUARD/AUTH: these are authenticated media pages — do they correctly fall under the SPA auth guard (i.e. NOT accidentally marked public)? Confirm they don't need meta and inherit the default guarded behavior.
3. NAV: are the 5 menu entries well-formed for the MenuItem type? Will `Music -> /app/music` resolve (built-in route exists)? Any entry pointing at a path with no route?
4. phpstan fix: is the is_scalar guard on the `id` line correct and consistent with sibling lines? Does it actually clear the cast.string error without changing behavior for normal string ids?
5. Any regression: did editing main.ts break the existing buildAdminRoutes()/LibraryScanPage spread or imports? Any duplicate import?
6. Note anything that will only truly work AFTER the release train rebuilds the web-ui bundle (expected — the source is committed, bundle deferred).

Run (read-only):
- `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && vendor/bin/phpstan analyse src/Server/WebPortal/WebPortalRouter.php 2>&1 | tail -8`
- `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server/web-ui && npx vue-tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json 2>&1 | tail -20`
- Inspect route-name hard-coding: `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server/web-ui && grep -rn "name: 'book-detail'\|name: 'music-artist'\|name: 'photo-view'\|'photo/photo'\|photo-view" node_modules/@phlix/ui/dist 2>/dev/null | head` (or check the .d.ts / compiled chunks).

Report a NUMBERED findings list (severity, file:line, description, suggested fix) OR literal `NO FINDINGS`. Focus on route-name/path mismatches (the highest-risk failure mode) and guard correctness.

## Summary

Fixed WS-D-HUB surface #7 — the broken public invite-acceptance flow (`GET /invite/{token}` → `/app/invite/{token}` had no Vue route → 404).

### Files created/changed (absolute paths)

phlix-ui (commit `68c25902b924085ce61b01e7af3c5bc09608c5f8`, pushed):
- `/home/sites/phlix/phlix-ui/src/pages/AcceptInvitePage.vue` (new) — the public accept page.

- `/home/sites/phlix/phlix-ui/src/index.ts:278` — exports `AcceptInvitePage`.
- `/home/sites/phlix/phlix-ui/src/components/LoginForm.vue` — added `useRoute` + `safeRedirect`; after successful login honors `route.query.redirect` via `router.replace` when safe, else `router.push(homePath)`.
- `/home/sites/phlix/phlix-ui/src/components/SignupForm.vue` — same redirect handling as LoginForm.

phlix-hub (commit `1b75b98cb1635f751d4afabfd9bffddc8c97152a`, pushed):
- `/home/sites/phlix/phlix-hub/web-ui/src/main.ts` — imports `AcceptInvitePage`, registers `{ path: '/app/invite/:token(.*)', name: 'accept-invite', component: AcceptInvitePage, props: true, meta: { public: true } }`.

### Auth flow implemented (parity with retired Smarty `accept-invite.tpl`/`.js`)
- Not logged in: generic "You've been invited to access a library" + Log In / Sign Up buttons that push `{ path: '/app/login'|'/app/signup', query: { redirect: '/

GOAL: The old Smarty `/settings/security` passkey page must be reachable in `/app`. `phlix-ui/src/pages/SecuritySettingsPage.vue` exists and is complete (loadCredentials via GET /api/v1/me/webauthn/credentials, register via navigator.credentials + /api/v1/auth/webauthn/register/*, delete-with-confirm). It is NOT wired anywhere. Make it a "Security" tab in `SettingsPage.vue`.

READ FIRST:
- `phlix-ui/src/pages/SettingsPage.vue` — it uses a shared `Tabs` primitive with a `TABS: TabItem[]` array (~lines 20-26: appearance/playback/server) and named slots (~37-45). Confirm the exact TabItem shape (value/label/icon) and slot mechanism.
- `phlix-ui/src/pages/SecuritySettingsPage.vue` — confirm it's self-contained (no required props, or a `client` prop with a default).
- CRITICAL — determine WHERE SettingsPage.vue is rendered: is it SERVER-ONLY, or shared by BOTH the phlix-server AND phlix-hub apps? Grep both consumers: `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server/web-ui && grep -rn "SettingsPage\|settings" src/main.ts` and check phlix-ui `createPhlixApp.ts` route table for the settings route, and whether phlix-hub uses SettingsPage at all. The WebAuthn endpoints (/api/v1/me/webauthn/*) are SERVER-side. If SettingsPage is shared and the hub renders it, a hub user would get a Security tab hitting a non-existent hub endpoint. Decide the SAFE approach:
   * If SettingsPage is server-only (hub has its own settings) → just add the tab unconditionally.
   * If SettingsPage is shared → gate the Security tab so it only appears where WebAuthn is supported (e.g. behind a config flag / capability already present in the app config, or a prop on SettingsPage, or feature-detect). Prefer the SIMPLEST correct option; report what you chose and why. Do NOT introduce a Security tab that 404s on the hub.

IMPLEMENT:
1. Add a `security` tab to `SettingsPage.vue`'s TABS array: `{ value: 'security', label: t('settings.tabSecurity'), icon: 'key' }` (use an icon name that exists in the icon set — verify; if 'key' isn't available pick an existing lock/shield icon).
2. Import `SecuritySettingsPage` (from `./SecuritySettingsPage.vue` or the package index — match how sibling tab panels are imported) and add a named slot `<template #security><SecuritySettingsPage /></template>`.
3. Add the i18n key `settings.tabSecurity` (value e.g. "Security") to the locale file(s) — find the en locale used for the other `settings.tab*` keys and add it there (and any other locales if the repo requires all locales to have parity — check).
4. (Optional but recommended) also register a deep-link route `/app/settings/security` that opens SettingsPage on the security tab, IF SettingsPage supports an initial-tab via route/query and doing so is low-risk. If it's non-trivial, skip and note it (the tab itself is the primary requirement).
5. Apply the tab-gating decision from the READ step so it never appears where webauthn is unsupported.

VERIFY (paste ACTUAL output):
- `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-ui && npx vue-tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | tail -25` (clean).
- `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-ui && npm run test:run 2>&1 | tail -20` (must not ADD failures; NOTE there are 5 KNOWN pre-existing failures in src/app/admin.test.ts + src/app/AdminLayout.test.ts about an `admin-requests` hub admin page — those are unrelated to you and will be fixed separately; confirm your change adds none beyond those).
- Do NOT run `npm run build`.

COMMIT: `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-ui && git add -A && git commit -m "feat(settings): add Security (passkeys/WebAuthn) tab to SettingsPage" && git push origin master`. Report: files changed (file:line), the SettingsPage-consumer finding + your gating decision + why, whether you added the deep-link route, i18n keys added, actual typecheck+test output (with the failure count and confirmation you added zero new failures), commit hash. READ-ONLY review. Do NOT Edit/Write/commit/mutate anything. Do NOT run `caliber refresh`. Always `cd /home/sites/phlix/<repo> && <cmd>` in the same command.

Review WS-D-HUB surface #7 fix. Two commits: phlix-ui `68c25902` and phlix-hub `1b75b98c`.
- `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-ui && git show 68c25902`
- `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-hub && git show 1b75b98c`

Context: Public invite acceptance was broken (`GET /invite/{token}` → `/app/invite/{token}` 404, no Vue page). This adds `phlix-ui/src/pages/AcceptInvitePage.vue`, a `safeRedirect` util, honors `route.query.redirect` in LoginForm/SignupForm, and registers the hub route `/app/invite/:token(.*)` with `meta:{public:true}`.

Review for:
1. SECURITY — `phlix-ui/src/utils/safeRedirect.ts`: scrutinize for open-redirect bypass. It should ONLY allow internal SPA paths starting with `/app/` and reject absolute URLs (`http://`, `https://`), protocol-relative (`//host`), backslash tricks (`/\evil`, `/app/\/evil`), whitespace/control-char tricks, and anything that could reach `window.location`. Confirm navigation uses `router.replace`/`router.push` (SPA) NOT `window.location`, so even a weird internal path is just a route (not an external redirect). Is the `/app/` prefix check sufficient, or can `/app/`-prefixed input still escape (e.g. `/app/../..`, encoded `%2f%2f`)? Give a decisive verdict: is the guard safe?
2. AUTH FLOW CORRECTNESS — AcceptInvitePage: logged-in → POST /api/v1/me/invite-links/{token}/redeem, success → /app/shared-with-me, errors 401/404/410/other mapped. Logged-out → Login/Signup with redirect back to the invite. Is `isLoggedIn` read correctly from the auth store? On the PUBLIC route the auth guard skips `auth.init()` — does the page still behave correctly if a stale token is present (surfaces as 401 on redeem)? Is `token` correctly `encodeURIComponent`-ed for the API call while the route uses `:token(.*)`?
3. ROUTE — hub `main.ts`: `{ path: '/app/invite/:token(.*)', name:'accept-invite', component: AcceptInvitePage, props:true, meta:{public:true} }`. Does `props:true` pass the catch-all `:token` correctly to the page prop? Does `meta:{public:true}` actually opt out of the guard (verify against createPhlixApp.ts guard logic ~71)? Does the catch-all `(.*)` risk swallowing other `/app/invite/...` intents (there are none)? Any conflict with the hub redirect at Application.php:1080 (should now resolve — confirm it still points to /app/invite/{token})?
4. REGRESSION — LoginForm/SignupForm: does honoring redirect BREAK the normal no-redirect login (must still go to config.home)? Is the redirect read reactively/correctly (route.query)? Any double-navigation?
5. Parity vs the retired Smarty accept-invite.tpl/js: same states/messages/behaviors?
6. vue-tsc / tests: `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-ui && npx vue-tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | tail -12`. Note: 5 pre-existing failures in src/app/admin.test.ts + AdminLayout.test.ts (about an `admin-requests` registry entry) are KNOWN/unrelated — ignore them, but CONFIRM this commit added no others.

Report a NUMBERED findings list (severity, file:line, description, suggested fix) OR literal `NO FINDINGS`. Be decisive on #1 (safeRedirect security).

 * This module is pure (no Vue, no DOM) so it is SSR-safe and trivially unit-tested.

 * and the `MessageKey` union stay simple and the merge is a single per-group spread.

<result>Review complete. All five functional areas plus the security guard and typecheck verified.

## Verdict on #1 (safeRedirect security): SAFE — decisively.

`/home/sites/phlix/phlix-ui/src/utils/safeRedirect.ts` is a sound open-redirect guard, and the design is defense-in-depth on two independent layers:

1. The value must be a non-empty `string` (arrays/undefined from `route.query.redirect` return `null`), must NOT start with `//` or `/\`, and MUST start with `/app/`. That rejects absolute URLs (`https://evil`), protocol-relative (`//evil`), and backslash tricks (`/\evil`).
2. Consumption is SPA-only: `LoginForm.vue:60` / `SignupForm.vue:80` feed the result exclusively to `router.replace(redirect)` — never `window.location` (confirmed by grep; the redirect value has zero `window.location` consumers). Vue Router resolves a string location as an in-app path against the current origin; it cannot cross origins even for adversarial input like `/app/%2f%2fevil` or `/app/../..`. So any `/app/`-prefixed weirdness that slips past layer 1 is still just an internal (likely 404) route, not an external redirect.

Encoded escapes (`%2f%2f…`) don't bypass: the check runs on the decoded `route.query.redirect` string, and even if they did, router navigation stays same-origin. The `/app/` prefix check is sufficient given the SPA-navigation constraint.

## Other areas — all clean:
- **#2 Auth flow:** `isLoggedIn` correctly reads `auth.isLoggedIn`

## general-purpose — Smarty-vs-/app reachability & parity investigation — 2026-07-18

READ-ONLY investigation of `/home/sites/phlix/phlix-hub`. No code changed.

### Root-path behavior
- `src/Application.php:328` — `GET /` → `(new Response())->redirect('/app/servers')`. The root is a hard redirect into the SPA. The Smarty landing `PageController::home()` / `home/index.tpl` is now UNREACHABLE (no route dispatches `/` to PageController).
- `/app` + `/app/{path:.*}` served by `SharedUiController::shell()` (Application.php:343-349; controller `src/Http/Controllers/SharedUiController.php`) — reads `public/assets/app/index.html`, injects `window.__PHLIX__`.

All render via `PageController` → `PageRenderer::render()` (`src/Common/WebPortal/PageRenderer.php`, Smarty `->fetch()`).

import type { RouteRecordRaw } from 'vue-router';
import type { MenuItem } from './types';
import type { IconName } from '../components/Icon.vue';

/**
 * Admin routes + menu seam (RA — admin port; H0 — composable page groups).
 *
 * The shared admin surface is an **opt-in, per-consumer** section: a host app
 * spreads `buildAdminRoutes()` (server) or `buildHubAdminRoutes()` (hub) into its
 * `PhlixAppConfig.extraRoutes`. The pages nest under a single parent route that
 * renders {@link AdminLayout} (a sidebar of the admin links + a `<RouterView>`),
 * so the admin section gets its own navigation chrome without the host shell
 * having to render a nested menu. Every page — and the layout itself — is a
 * lazily-imported chunk, so a consumer that never shows admin doesn't pay for it.
 *
 * **Page groups (H0).** Admin pages are exported as three registries so each app
 * mounts only the surfaces it has a backend for:
 * - {@link commonAdminPages} — portable to BOTH apps: Users, Logs, Settings.
 * - {@link serverAdminPages} — media-server-only: Dashboard, Webhooks, Services,
 *   Integrations, Backup, Cast Devices, DLNA, Remote Access, Live TV, Collections,
 *   History, SyncPlay, Libraries, Duplicates, Plugins.
 * - {@link hubAdminPages} — hub-only: Hub Dashboard, Audit Logs.
 *
 * `buildAdminRoutes(base)` with no `pages` argument yields the **server pages in
 * their original sidebar order** (Dashboard first), so the server's admin landing
 * page and sidebar ordering are unchanged (U6 adds Plugins after Libraries).
 * `buildServerAdminRoutes` is the explicit synonym for that default;
 * `buildHubAdminRoutes` mounts the hub set (Hub Dashboard, Users, Logs, Settings,
 * Audit Logs). Each child keeps a stable `admin-*` route name and resolves to
 *

<script setup lang="ts">
/**
 * AcceptInvitePage (WS-D-HUB #7) — the PUBLIC invite-acceptance surface.
 *
 * Reached at `/app/invite/:token`, where the hub redirects the public
 * `GET /invite/{token}` link. This is the only genuinely broken hub surface: the
 * PHP redirect had no matching Vue route (404). This restores parity with the
 * retired Smarty `home/accept-invite.tpl` + `accept-invite.js` flow:
 *
 *   - NOT logged in → generic "you've been invited" message + Log In / Sign Up
 *     buttons that carry a `?redirect=/app/invite/<token>` hop back here (honoured
 *     by LoginForm/SignupForm's safe-redirect guard) so the user returns to accept.
 *   - Logged in → an "Accept Invite" button → `POST /api/v1/me/invite-links/{token}/redeem`
 *     (auth-gated). On success a confirmation + a "View Shared Libraries" button
 *     to `/app/shared-with-me`. Errors map: 401 re-login, 404 not found, 410
 *     expired/exhausted, else generic.
 *
 * The route is `meta: { public: true }` so the auth guard lets an unauthenticated
 * visitor reach it. `isLoggedIn` here is only a token-PRESENCE flag (the public
 * route skips `auth.init()`); a stale token simply surfaces as a 401 on redeem,
 * which routes the user to log in again — matching the Smarty behaviour.
 *
 * `client` is an injectable test seam; it defaults to the shared `api` singleton.
 */

import { useRouter } from 'vue-router';
import { api, ApiClient } from '../api/client';

import Button from '../components/ui/Button.vue';

4. `AudiobooksPage.vue` — grid load (GET /api/v1/audiobooks); empty/error; navigate to `audiobook-detail`.
5. `AudiobookDetailPage.vue` — load by :id; metadata + chapter list; Play → `audiobook-player`; Download.
6. `AudiobookPlayerPage.vue` — load; play/pause, skip -30/+30, chapter list active state, progress save (POST /api/v1/audiobooks/{id}/progress). This is a large component (~854 lines) — cover load + core transport controls + progress save; don't chase every branch.
7. `SearchPage.vue` — debounced query synced to `?q=`; calls GET /api/v1/media/search; prompt empty-state (no query) vs no-results vs results grid. Use fake timers if needed for the debounce, but flush them deterministically.

import { watch, onScopeDispose, type Ref, type ComputedRef } from 'vue';

/**
 * usePageTitle (U1) — centralized `document.title` management for the SPA.
 *
 * The site sets no document title on its own; this module is the single place
 * that formats and writes it. Titles read `"<title> · Phlix"` (or just the app
 * name when the page-specific part is empty), so the separator + suffix + app
 * name live in exactly one spot.
 *
 * Two entry points:
 * - {@link setPageTitle} — imperative, used by the router `afterEach` hook (for
 *   static/route-default titles) and by pages that resolve their title from
 *   async-loaded data (media/series name, library name, the playing item).
 * - {@link usePageTitle} — a small composable that watches a ref/getter and
 *   keeps the document title in sync while the owning component is mounted.
 *
 * SSR-safe: every `document` access is guarded behind `typeof document`, so
 * importing/using this in a non-browser context is a no-op rather than a throw.
 */

/** The separator between the page-specific part and the app-name suffix. */
const SEPARATOR = ' · ';

/** Default app name (wordmark) for the title suffix. Overridable per app via
 *  {@link setAppName} (the host's `branding.wordmark`). */
const DEFAULT_APP_NAME = 'Phlix';

/** Module-level app name used as the title suffix. Mutable so a host app can set
 *  its own wordmark once at boot (createPhlixApp) without threading it through
 *  every `setPageTitle` call site. */
let appName = DEFAULT_APP_NAME;

/**
 * Set the app name used as the title suffix (e.g. from `branding.wordmark`).
 * Called once by `createPhlixApp` at boot. A blank value falls back to the
 * default so the suffix is never empty.
 */
export function setAppName(nam

cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-ui && git commit -q -F - <<'EOF' && git rev-parse HEAD
test(ws-d-srv): specs for Books/Audiobooks/Search SPA pages

Add Vitest specs for the newly-routable Books, Audiobooks and Search
pages (loading/empty/error/populated states + key actions/navigation),
mounting against a stubbed global fetch + memory router.

Also fix a temporal-dead-zone crash the specs surfaced: BookDetailPage,
BookReaderPage, AudiobookDetailPage and AudiobookPlayerPage called
usePageTitle(() => x.value?.name) BEFORE declaring `const x = ref(...)`,
so the immediate title watcher read the ref in its TDZ and threw on
mount (these pages were unreachable until routing was just added, so it
was never exercised). Reorder to declare the ref first, matching
MediaDetailPage.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01TaV3FqfbCcDXkZR6af1fyK
EOF

These pages were unreachable until routing was added, so such bugs were never exercised. WATCH FOR THE SAME CLASS OF BUG in the pages below: if mounting a page throws (TDZ ref-before-declaration, calling a composable with a getter over a not-yet-declared ref, undefined store, etc.), FIX the source (reorder decls / mirror the canonical MediaDetailPage.vue ordering) — a real mount crash is a source defect, not a test artifact. Report any such fix explicitly.

GOAL: Add Vitest specs (mount + mocked client/api seam; deterministic, no real HTTP, flush any timers) for these already-built pages. READ each component first to assert what it REALLY does. Match existing style (sibling page tests). Cover loading/empty/error/populated + key actions:
1. `PhotoAlbumsPage.vue` — GET photo albums (src/api/photos.ts); grid of albums (cover/date/count); empty/error; click album → navigate to `/app/photo/album/{id}` (path-based).
2. `PhotoAlbumPage.vue` — load album by :id; photo grid; back link; click photo → `/app/photo/photo/{id}`.
3. `PhotoViewPage.vue` — load full image + EXIF panel (assert the EXIF fields the component renders: date-taken/camera/lens/focal/aperture/shutter/ISO/dimensions/GPS as present); Download Full Size; Slideshow button; prev/next photo nav.
4. `PhotoSlideshowPage.vue` — reads `?library_id/album/interval` query; prev/next/play-pause/exit; counter (n/total); thumbnail strip; interval auto-advance (fake timers, flush deterministically).
5. `SecuritySettingsPage.vue` — loadCredentials (GET /api/v1/me/webauthn/credentials): list/empty/error; Delete with confirm (mock window.confirm) → DELETE + toast; Register flow (mock navigator.credentials.create + the register options/verify endpoints) — assert the round-trip calls; device-type icon/label.
6. `MusicArtistsPage.vue` — GET artists; grid; empty/error; click → `music-artist` with :name.
7. `MusicArtistPage.vue` — prop `name` (props:true); load artist's albums; click album → `music-album` with :name; back link.
8. `MusicAlbumPage.vue` — prop `name` (props:true); load album tracks; play a track; back link `/app/music`.
9. `MusicTracksPage.vue` — GET /api/v1/music/tracks; search/filter/pagination; play a track; empty/error.
10. `MusicPlayerPage.vue` — consumes the shared useMusicPlayer store (mock it); renders now-playing; transport controls (play/pause/next/prev); no route param.

VERIFY (paste ACTUAL output):
- `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-ui && npx vue-tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | tail -20`
- `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-ui && npm run test:run 2>&1 | tail -20` — must be FULLY GREEN (0 failures). Report exact totals (files/tests/passed/failed/skipped).
- Do NOT run `npm run build`.

COMMIT: `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-ui && git add -A && git commit -m "test(ws-d-srv): specs for Photo/Music/Security SPA pages (+ any mount-crash fixes)" && git push origin master`. Report: files added/changed (paths), any SOURCE mount-crash bugs you found+fixed (this is important — call them out), per-page key assertions, pages covered partially + why, full final test totals, commit hash. No flaky tests. Report is DATA for the parent.

 * Music playback composable — gapless + crossfade, fully client-side.
 *
 * Drives two alternating `<audio>` elements whose `src` is a track's
 * server-minted **signed** `stream_url` (`/media/:id/stream?exp&sig`, the field
 * emitted by the server's `formatTrack`; see UI-3.6 / X8). No Bearer header is
 * needed — the signature authorizes the byte stream — so the URL works directly
 * as an `<audio src>`.
 *
 * Crossfade and gapless are implemented with NO server dependency:
 *   - **Gapless:** the next track's `stream_url` is pre-loaded onto the idle
 *     `<audio>` element (`preload='auto'`) so the swap at track-end is instant.
 *   - **Crossfade:** the idle element starts at `volume = 0` while the active
 *     element ramps down, stepping both volumes over `crossfadeDuration` so the
 *     two overlap-fade. The server `buildGaplessSegmentCommand` path is NOT used.

 * Both settings are read live from {@link usePreferencesStore}, so the existing
 * crossfade/gapless settings UI finally takes effect.
 *

Documentation scribe for WS-D (Smarty→/app SPA migration). Make NO behavioral code changes (docblocks/comments only in code, and you may add code docblocks if missing). Always `cd /home/sites/phlix/<repo> && <cmd>` in the same command. Do NOT run `caliber refresh`. No PRs. Commit directly to master. You are the only writer on these repos right now.

CRITICAL FRAMING: The Smarty stack was NOT deleted — deletion is intentionally DEFERRED until the owner verifies the new /app pages live. So docs must describe the new SPA pages/routes as AVAILABLE, but must NOT claim Smarty is removed/retired/deleted. Frame it as "migrated to /app (Smarty retirement pending verification)".

WHAT SHIPPED IN WS-D (verify against actual code before writing):
- phlix-hub (D-HUB): 6 owner/user surfaces already had /app Vue pages (requests, admin request-approval, shared-with-me, invite-links, server-detail, federation-shares). NEW this round: public invite acceptance page `AcceptInvitePage.vue` (route `/app/invite/:token(.*)`, hub `web-ui/src/main.ts`), fixing the previously-broken `/invite/{token}` 404. LoginForm/SignupForm now honor a safe internal `?redirect` (new `safeRedirect` util). Vitest specs added for all these pages.
- phlix-ui (shared @phlix/ui): new `AcceptInvitePage.vue`, `safeRedirect.ts`; new "Security" (WebAuthn/passkeys) tab in `SettingsPage.vue`; ~15 new page test specs. (These pages: Books/Audiobooks/Photo/Search/Music-subpages/Security already existed but are now covered by tests + reachable.) NOTE a real crash-on-mount bug was fixed in BookDetailPage/BookReaderPage/AudiobookDetailPage/AudiobookPlayerPage (usePageTitle called before the ref was declared → TDZ ReferenceError on mount).
- phlix-server (D-SRV): `web-ui/src/main.ts` now registers routes + nav for the previously-unreachable SPA pages — Books (`/app/books`, `/app/books/:id`, `/app/books/:id/read`), Audiobooks (`/app/audiobooks`, `/:id`, `/:id/play`), Photo (`/app/photo/albums`, `/album/:id`, `/photo/:id`, `/slideshow`), Search (`/app/search`), Music sub-pages (`/app/music/artists`, `/artist/:name`, `/album/:name`, `/tracks`, `/player`), plus nav entries (Music/Books/Audiobooks/Photos/Search). Also fixed a phpstan cast in `getMusicTracks` (WebPortalRouter.php). The passkey page is now a Settings "Security" tab.

DOCUMENTATION TASKS (check current file contents + style first; match each repo):
1. `phlix-ui/CHANGELOG.md` — add entries under the unreleased section: AcceptInvitePage + safeRedirect (public invite acceptance + safe login redirect); Settings Security/passkeys tab; the Book/Audiobook mount-crash fix; the new page test coverage.
2. `phlix-hub/CHANGELOG.md` + `phlix-hub/README.md` — document that the hub owner/user surfaces (requests, admin-requests, shared-with-me, invite-links, server-detail, federation-shares, public invite acceptance) are now served by the /app SPA; the `/app/invite/:token` public route. Do NOT claim Smarty removed. If README documents routes/features, update accordingly.
3. `phlix-server/CHANGELOG.md` + `phlix-server/README.md` — document the newly-reachable /app pages (Books/Audiobooks/Photo/Search/Music sub-pages) + nav, the Settings Security tab, and the getMusicTracks phpstan fix. Do NOT claim Smarty removed.
4. `phlix-docs/docs/` — find the right page(s) (UI/SPA overview, or per-feature pages) and document that these surfaces are available under /app. If there's a natural "SPA pages" or "web UI" doc, update it; otherwise add concise notes where they fit. You are the ONLY phlix-docs writer now — commit promptly.
5. Docblocks: ensure `safeRedirect.ts` and `AcceptInvitePage.vue` have brief accurate docblocks (they likely do — verify). Code docblocks/comments only.

Keep a consistent "migration to /app; Smarty retirement pending verification" framing everywhere.

VERIFY nothing broke (docblock edits only): `cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-ui && npx vue-tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | tail -8` (only if you edited any .ts/.vue; skip if markdown-only).

COMMIT per repo you touched (only repos you changed):
- phlix-ui: `git add -A && git commit -m "docs(ws-d): AcceptInvitePage/safeRedirect, Settings Security tab, page test coverage" && git push origin master`
- phlix-hub: `git add -A && git commit -m "docs(ws-d): hub owner/user surfaces served by /app SPA + public invite route" && git push origin master`
- phlix-server: `git add -A && git commit -m "docs(ws-d): newly-reachable /app media pages + Settings Security tab" && git push origin master`
- phlix-docs: `git add -A && git commit -m "docs(ws-d): /app SPA surfaces (hub + server) availability" && git push origin master`

Report: files changed per repo (file:line), what you documented, any skips + why, verification output, commit hashes. Report is DATA for the parent.

<script setup lang="ts">
/**
 * AcceptInvitePage (WS-D-HUB #7) — the PUBLIC invite-acceptance surface.
 *
 * Reached at `/app/invite/:token`, where the hub redirects the public
 * `GET /invite/{token}` link. This is the only genuinely broken hub surface: the
 * PHP redirect had no matching Vue route (404). This restores parity with the
 * retired Smarty `home/accept-invite.tpl` + `accept-invite.js` flow:
 *
 *   - NOT logged in → generic "you've been invited" message + Log In / Sign Up
 *     buttons that carry a `?redirect=/app/invite/<token>` hop back here (honoured
 *     by LoginForm/SignupForm's safe-redirect guard) so the user returns to accept.
 *   - Logged in → an "Accept Invite" button → `POST /api/v1/me/invite-links/{token}/redeem`
 *     (auth-gated). On success a confirmation + a "View Shared Libraries" button
 *     to `/app/shared-with-me`. Errors map: 401 re-login, 404 not found, 410
 *     expired/exhausted, else generic.
 *
 * The route is `meta: { public: true }` so the auth guard lets an unauthenticated
 * visitor reach it. `isLoggedIn` here is only a token-PRESENCE flag (the public
 * route skips `auth.init()`); a stale token simply surfaces as a 401 on redeem,
 * which routes the user to log in again — matching the Smarty behaviour.
 *
 * `client` is an injectable test seam; it defaults to the shared `api` singleton.
 */
import { ref, computed } from 'vue';

<!--
 * @copyright 2026 Joe Huss <detain@interserver.net>
 * @license MIT
 -->

<script setup lang="ts">
/**
 * AcceptInvitePage (WS-D-HUB #7) — the PUBLIC invite-acceptance surface.
 *
 * Reached at `/app/invite/:token`, where the hub redirects the public
 * `GET /invite/{token}` link. This is the only genuinely broken hub surface: the
 * PHP redirect had no matching Vue route (404). This restores parity with the
 * retired Smarty `home/accept-invite.tpl` + `accept-invite.js` flow:
 *
 *   - NOT logged in → generic "you've been invited" message + Log In / Sign Up
 *     buttons that carry a `?redirect=/app/invite/<token>` hop back here (honoured
 *     by LoginForm/SignupForm's safe-redirect guard) so the user returns to accept.
 *   - Logged in → an "Accept Invite" button → `POST /api/v1/me/invite-links/{token}/redeem`
 *     (auth-gated). On success a confirmation + a "View Shared Libraries" button
 *     to `/app/shared-with-me`. Errors map: 401 re-login, 404 not found, 410
 *     expired/exhausted, else generic.
 *
 * The route is `meta: { public: true }` so the auth guard lets an unauthenticated
 * visitor reach it. `isLoggedIn` here is only a token-PRESENCE flag (the public
 * route skips `auth.init()`); a stale token simply surfaces as a 401 on redeem,
 * which routes the user to log in again — matching the Smarty behaviour.
 *
 * `client` is an injectable test seam; it defaults to the shared `api` singleton.
 */
import { ref, computed } from 'vue';

tool_input.command: cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-ui && head -40 CHANGELOG.md 2>/dev/null; echo "=====HUB CHANGELOG====="; cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-hub && head -40 CHANGELOG.md 2>/dev/null; echo "=====SRV CHANGELOG====="; cd /home/sites/phlix/phlix-server && head -40 CHANGELOG.md 2>/dev/null
tool_input.description: Read top of all three changelogs

tool_response._truncated:
{"stdout":"## Unreleased

### Changes
- chore(cleanup): remove the dead `src/api/music.ts` module (zero importers; `fetchArtists`/`fetchAlbumsByArtist`/`fetchTracksByAlbum` were superseded by the `ApiClient` music methods). Never bundled, so no `dist/` rebuild — internal source cleanup only.


## v0.82.0 (2026-07-18)

### Changes
- feat(admin/logs): overhaul the log renderer — emit separate **channel** and **level** badges (the full Monolog/PSR-3 level set mapped onto five colour tones), no more duplicated level text
- feat(admin/logs): show timestamps in the viewer's **local time** as `h:mm:ss.<micros>AM/PM` (12-hour), splicing sub-second precision back from the raw ISO (JS `Date` truncates to ms) and dropping the date when the entry is from today
- feat(admin/logs): strip the `-YYYY-MM-DD.log` rotation suffix from filenames (accepts both the Monolog hyphen separator and the legacy dot separator)
- feat(admin/logs): **cross-file line combine** now fires — the parser populates source/timestamp/message so identical lines across multiple files merge into a single row with a comma-joined source list
- fix(admin/logs): defensively strip a redundant legacy inline `[LEVEL] datetime ` prefix from message bodies
- fix(admin/logs): fix message **double-escape** (`highlightJson` already escapes `& < >`, so the message is no longer escaped twice) and HTML-escape the channel/source fragments before `v-html` (the XSS boundary for raw log content)
- test(player): refresh stale QualityMenu/quality/shortcuts specs left behind by earlier feature commits (closest-level-≥-source contract, the q/Q quality shortcut, and the variant-id emit); only a stale docstring changes in product code


## v0.81.0 (2026-07-18)

### Changes
- feat(detail): "Play Trailer" button with in-app YouTube embed modal (validated key/URL)
- feat(detail): title-logo hero overlay (falls back to text title; handles local PNG or remote SVG)
- feat(admin/profiles): content-rating cap picker

- feat(admin/logs): show timestamps in the viewer's **local time** as `h:mm:ss.<micros>AM/PM` (12-hour), splicing sub-second precision back from the raw ISO (JS `Date` truncates to ms) and dropping the date when the entry is from today

- test(player): refresh stale QualityMenu/quality/shortcuts specs left behind by earlier feature commits (closest-level-≥-source contract, the q/Q quality shortcut, and the variant-id emit); only a stale docstring changes in product code


## v0.81.0 (2026-07-18)

426:| GET | `/api/v1/users/me/continue-watching` (also `/api/v1/me/continue-watching`) | Continue-Watching list for the authenticated user — `{items:[...]}` of `MediaItemShaper`-shaped rows with top-level `id` (= media item id, for detail navigation), `poster_url`/`poster_srcset` (series poster for episodes, falling back season → own), `runtime` (minutes), `year`, `rating`, plus re-attached `position_ticks`/`duration_ticks` (SPA resume) and preserved `media_item_id`, `parent_id`, `metadata` (console/rating-gate compatibility) |

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sites/phlix/phlix-hub/README.md","content":"- **Invite links** — single-use, signed invite links that grant library access to a recipient.
- **Hub-to-hub federation** — federate with peer hubs to share libraries across hubs, with
  per-peer sessions and admin delegation.
- **Media requests** — a Jellyseerr-class request queue. Users request movies/series; admins
  approve, and the Hub talks to Sonarr/Radarr to fulfil them.
- **Web UI & admin console** — the hub's front door is a Vue SPA (the shared `@phlix/ui` design
  system) served at `/app` (`/` redirects to `/app/servers`): My Servers, Federation, and Shares for
  every signed-in user, plus a `requiresAdmin` **admin console** at `/app/admin/*` (Hub Dashboard,
  Users, Logs, Settings, Audit Logs). The original Smarty pages are deprecated and will be removed
  in a future release. Everything is backed by a full JSON API under `/api/v1` (incl. `/api/v1/admin/*`).
- **Operations-ready** — structured JSON logging (Monolog) across dedicated channels
  (app, error, hub, relay, audit), a `/health` endpoint, and idempotent SQL migrations.

## Architecture

The Hub runs as a set of long-lived [Workerman](https://www.workerman.net/) workers in a single
process group:

| Worker | Default port | Purpose |
|--------|--------------|---------|
| HTTP | `8800` | REST API + the Vue SPA (`/app`) + legacy SSR pages + `/health` |
| Relay (server-facing) | `8802` | Servers connect here to open their outbound tunnel |
| Relay (client-facing) | `8803` | Remote clients connect (`GET /client/{server_id}`) and are routed down a tunnel |

Supporting pieces:

- **PSR-11 container** (PHP-DI 7) wires services; routes are registered in
  [`src/Application.php`](src/Application.php).
- **MySQL** is accessed through an async connection pool (`workerman/mysql`). The pool is
  initialised lazily so `/health` stays up even if the database is briefly unreachable.","numLines":30,"s

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sites/phlix/phlix-hub/README.md","content":"| `GET` | `/api/v1/servers/{id}/info` | Server metadata (enrollment JWT) |
| `POST`/`DELETE` | `/servers/{id}/subdomain` | Allocate / revoke subdomain |

### Sharing, invites & requests

| Method | Path | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|
| `POST`/`GET` | `/api/v1/me/shares` | Create / list library shares |
| `PATCH`/`DELETE` | `/api/v1/me/shares/{id}` | Update / delete a share |
| `POST`/`GET` | `/api/v1/me/invite-links` | Create / list invite links |
| `POST` | `/api/v1/me/invite-links/{token}/redeem` | Redeem an invite link and create a library share (protected) |
| `GET` | `/invite/{token}` | Accept an invite (public page) |
| `POST`/`GET` | `/api/v1/me/requests` | Create / list media requests |
| `GET` | `/api/v1/admin/requests` | Admin request queue |
| `POST` | `/api/v1/admin/requests/{id}/approve` | Approve a request |
| `POST` | `/api/v1/admin/requests/{id}/deny` | Deny a request |

### Admin (web console)

The Vue admin console at `/app/admin/*` is backed by these admin-gated endpoints
(`[AuthMiddleware, AdminMiddleware]` — 401 unauthenticated / 403 non-admin):

| Method | Path | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|
| `GET` | `/api/v1/admin/dashboard/summary` | Server fleet (total/online/offline), active relay sessions, pending requests, user count |
| `GET` | `/api/v1/admin/dashboard/activity` | Recent audit events (`?limit=`) |
| `GET`/`POST` | `/api/v1/admin/users` | List / create accounts |
| `GET`/`PUT`/`DELETE` | `/api/v1/admin/users/{id}` | Fetch / update / delete an account |
| `POST` | `/api/v1/admin/users/{id}/set-admin` | Grant / revoke admin |
| `POST` | `/api/v1/admin/users/{id}/reset-password` | Set a new password |
| `GET` | `/api/v1/admin/logs`, `/logs/tail`, `/logs/tail-all` | Browse / tail the hub log files |
| `GET`/`PUT` | `/api/v1/admin/settings` | Read / persist hub settings |
| `GET` | `/api/v1/admin/users/{id}/bandwidth`

## general-purpose — WS-D-SRV routing/nav/parity/phpstan spec — 2026-07-18

READ-ONLY audit. No files mutated. Verified against actual Smarty .tpl + Vue .vue + route tables + server routes.

### Headline findings
- Every Vue page (Books/Audiobooks/Photo/Search/Security/Music sub-pages) is EXPORTED from
  `phlix-ui/src/index.ts` (lines 269, 278-292) and is a FEATURE SUPERSET of its Smarty original.
  The Vue pages are 250-854 lines vs 15-224-line Smarty templates. NO material feature-parity gaps found.
- NONE of these routes are registered anywhere (buildRoutes, admin.ts, or server web-ui main.ts).
  All server media surfaces are currently unreachable in `/app`.
- Route-registration mechanism = add imports + route objects to
  `phlix-server/web-ui/src/main.ts` `extraRoutes` (server-only, mirrors the existing LibraryScanPage
  precedent). `/music` is the lone exception already in shared `buildRoutes()`.
- Passkeys: recommend adding a 4th tab to `SettingsPage.vue` (matches Smarty `/settings/security`),
  not a standalone route.
- phpstan error WebPortalRouter.php:2884 = `(string)($row['id'] ?? '')` casting mixed. Fix with is_scalar guard.
- Zero vitest specs exist for any of these pages (MusicLibraryPage has one, but the sub-pages do not).

- Photo = PATH-based (no route names): /app/photo/albums, /app/photo/album/:id, /app/photo/photo/:id, /app/photo/slideshow
- music-artist (:name, props:true), music-album (:name, props:true); MusicTracksPage/MusicPlayerPage standalone
- Search = path /app/search?q=... (name 'search'); Secu

file.content:
and [phlix-docs / admin / server-settings](https://detain.github.io/phlix-docs/admin/server-settings).

### Web Portal
- **Smarty-based Templates**: Server-side rendered HTML pages using Smarty
- **REST API Endpoints**: Complete API for library browsing, media info, and user data
- **JWT Authentication**: Integrated token-based auth with refresh support
- **Responsive Design**: CSS-first approach with utility classes
- **JavaScript Client**: ApiClient helper with auth, library, and player helpers
- **Continue Watching**: Track and display in-progress media
- **Library Browser**: Browse media by library with item counts

### Authentication & Security
- **JWT-based Authentication**: Stateless auth with access tokens (1 hour TTL) and refresh tokens (7 days TTL)
- **Secure Password Hashing**: Argon2ID for password storage
- **Multi-Device Sessions**: Track and manage sessions across devices
- **User Profiles**: Multiple profiles per account with parental controls
  - Up to 5 profiles per user account
  - Profile-specific content rating restrictions (G, PG, PG-13, R, NC-17, X, UNRATED)
  - PIN protection (4 or 6 digits) for profile settings
  - Genre-based filtering (allowed/blocked genre lists)

and [phlix-docs / admin / server-settings](https://detain.github.io/phlix-docs/admin/server-settings).

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sites/phlix/phlix-server/README.md","content":"`php bin/phlix migrate` is the supported equivalent of `php scripts/run-migrations.php` — both delegate to the same `Phlix\Common\Database\MigrationRunner` service, applying every `migrations/*.sql` file on each run (idempotent; no tracking table). More commands are added in later steps.

### Admin SPA (admin-ui)

The admin console is a **React + TypeScript + Vite** single-page app. Its source lives in
`admin-ui/`; the production build is emitted into `public/assets/admin/` and **committed to the
repo**, so the running Workerman server has **no Node build dependency at runtime** (it just serves
the static shell + bundle). `admin-ui/node_modules/` is gitignored.

The SPA mounts at `/admin` and `/admin/*`, served by `AdminAppController` (returns the built
`index.html` shell; 503 if the bundle is missing) and gated by the existing `AdminMiddleware` — a
non-admin (401/403) is redirected (302) to `/login`. Its typed `ApiClient` reuses the same JWT
mechanism as `public/assets/js/api-client.js` (`access_token`/`refresh_token` in `localStorage`,
Bearer header, single retry on 401 via `POST /auth/refresh`).

```bash
cd admin-ui
npm install          # one-time / on dependency changes
npm run build        # tsc + vite build → ../public/assets/admin/ (commit the result)
npm run test         # Vitest unit/component tests
npm run dev          # Vite dev server (HMR) for local development
```

When you change anything under `admin-ui/src/`, re-run `npm run build` and commit the refreshed
`public/assets/admin/` bundle along with your source changes.

CI runs the SPA build + Vitest suite on any change under `admin-ui/` via the **Admin UI** GitHub
Actions workflow (`.github/workflows/admin-ui.yml`): `npm ci → npm run build → npm run test` on
push/PR to `master`/`main`/`develop` (path-filtered, so PHP-only changes don't trigger it).

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sites/phlix/phlix-docs/docs/clients/web.md","content":"# Web Portal

**Since:** 0.18.0

> [!TIP]
> The Phlix web portal runs in any modern browser — no software to install. Navigate to your server's web address, sign in with your Hub account or enter a direct server URL, and start streaming immediately. Works on Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge.

## Install / Store Links

- **No installation required** — open the URL in your browser and start using it
- **Supported browsers:** Chrome 110+, Firefox 115+, Safari 16+, Edge 110+
- **Mobile browsers:** Fully functional but optimized for desktop

Bookmark your server's web portal URL:
```
https://your-server-domain.com/web
```
Or for local access:
```
http://192.168.1.100:32400/web
```

## Platform-Specific Notes

- The web portal requires the server's web address to be reachable from your browser — either on the local network or via a Hub relay / reverse proxy.
- Some browser extensions (ad blockers, privacy extensions) may interfere with playback. If playback does not start, try disabling extensions or using an incognito/private window.
- For best playback performance, use a browser with hardware acceleration enabled (Chrome and Edge have this on by default).
- **Incompatible formats play automatically.** Titles your browser can't play directly — non-web containers like MKV, or codecs like HEVC (including **10-bit HEVC**) — are transcoded on the server on demand and streamed as HLS. The server transcodes them to 8-bit H.264 (High@4.1, yuv420p) so they decode in any modern browser. You'll see a brief "Preparing your stream…" message while the server starts the conversion, then playback begins. mp4/WebM titles play instantly with no conversion.

## Setup Steps

### Open the Web Portal

1. Open your browser and navigate to your server's web URL.
2. You land on the Phlix login screen.

### Sign In With Hub (Recommended for Multi-Server Users)
\

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sites/phlix/phlix-docs/docs/clients/web.md","content":"# Web Portal

**Since:** 0.18.0

> [!TIP]
> The Phlix web portal runs in any modern browser — no software to install. Navigate to your server's web address, sign in with your Hub account or enter a direct server URL, and start streaming immediately. Works on Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge.

## Install / Store Links

- **No installation required** — open the URL in your browser and start using it
- **Supported browsers:** Chrome 110+, Firefox 115+, Safari 16+, Edge 110+
- **Mobile browsers:** Fully functional but optimized for desktop

Bookmark your server's web portal URL:
```
https://your-server-domain.com/web
```
Or for local access:
```
http://192.168.1.100:32400/web
```

## Platform-Specific Notes

- The web portal requires the server's web address to be reachable from your browser — either on the local network or via a Hub relay / reverse proxy.
- Some browser extensions (ad blockers, privacy extensions) may interfere with playback. If playback does not start, try disabling extensions or using an incognito/private window.
- For best playback performance, use a browser with hardware acceleration enabled (Chrome and Edge have this on by default).
- **Incompatible formats play automatically.** Titles your browser can't play directly — non-web containers like MKV, or codecs like HEVC (including **10-bit HEVC**) — are transcoded on the server on demand and streamed as HLS. The server transcodes them to 8-bit H.264 (High@4.1, yuv420p) so they decode in any modern browser. You'll see a brief "Preparing your stream…" message while the server starts the conversion, then playback begins. mp4/WebM titles play instantly with no conversion.

## Setup Steps

### Open the Web Portal

1. Open your browser and navigate to your server's web URL.
2. You land on the Phlix login screen.

### Sign In With Hub (Recommended for Multi-Server Users)
\

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sites/phlix/phlix-docs/docs/hub/invite-links.md","content":"# Invite Links

**Since:** 0.19.0

## TL;DR

Invite links let you share access to a library (or all libraries) on one of your claimed servers with anyone — no hub account required on their end. You set the permission level, a maximum number of uses, and an expiry. The recipient visits the link, logs in or creates a hub account, and immediately sees the shared library under **"Shared with me"**.

---

## The Invite Links Page

Invite links are managed through the Hub API (the `/api/v1/me/invite-links`
endpoints below) and the legacy `/invite-links` page. They are not in the current
SPA top navigation (My Servers / Federation / Shares) — the public acceptance link
`/invite/{token}` that you send to recipients is unchanged.

The invite-links view lists every invite link you have created, one card per link:

```
┌─ Invite Link ──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Server: My Server                     [Copy URL] [✕]│
│ Library: All Libraries · Permission: Read · Uses: 0/1 │
│ Expires: Jun 5 2026 · Created: May 29 2026           │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Reading a link card

Each card shows:

- **Server** — the claimed server this link targets.
- **Copy URL** — copies the invite URL to your clipboard. The button briefly shows "Copied!" after a successful copy.
- **Revoke (✕)** — deletes the invite link immediately. The card disappears from the list and the URL no longer works.
- **Library** — either "All Libraries" or the specific library name. A link for all libraries is created when `library_id` is not set.
- **Permission** — `Read` or `Read/Write`. Shown as a coloured badge.
- **Uses** — `X / Y` where `Y` is the maximum uses you set and `X` is how many times the link has been used. A link with `X = Y` shows an **Exhausted** badge and the revoke button remains available.
- **Expiry*

## Unreleased

### Changes
- chore(cleanup): remove the dead `src/api/music.ts` module (zero importers; `fetchArtists`/`fetchAlbumsByArtist`/`fetchTracksByAlbum` were superseded by the `ApiClient` music methods). Never bundled, so no `dist/` rebuild — internal source cleanup only.
tool_input.new_string:
## Unreleased

### Changes
- feat(invite): new `AcceptInvitePage.vue` — the PUBLIC invite-acceptance surface reached at `/app/invite/:token`. Consumed by the hub (which redirects its public `GET /invite/{token}` link here) to restore the invite-accept flow the retired Smarty `accept-invite.tpl` provided: unauthenticated visitors get Log In / Sign Up buttons that carry a safe `?redirect` hop back to the invite; authenticated visitors get an **Accept Invite** button that calls `POST /api/v1/me/invite-links/{token}/redeem` and, on success, a **View Shared Libraries** link to `/app/shared-with-me`. Route is `meta.public` so the auth guard lets unauthenticated invitees through.
- feat(auth): new `safeRedirect(value)` util (`src/utils/safeRedirect.ts`) — an open-redirect guard that only honours same-origin `?redirect=` values under the SPA root `/app/` (rejecting absolute URLs, protocol-relative `//host`, and `/\host` backslash tricks). `LoginForm`/`SignupForm` now honour a validated internal `?redirect` so a post-login/signup hop returns the user to where they started (e.g. the invite page).
- feat(settings): new **Security** tab in `SettingsPage.vue` surfacing WebAuthn/passkey management (register/list/remove passkeys), replacing the standalone Smarty `settings/security` page.
- fix(reader): fix a crash-on-mount in `BookDetailPage`, `BookReaderPage`, `AudiobookDetailPage`, and `AudiobookPlayerPage` — `usePageTitle()` was called before the reactive title ref was declared, tripping a temporal-dead-zone `ReferenceError` on mount. These pages now mount cleanly (they are the media surfaces the server now registers under `/app`).
- test(pages): add Vitest specs covering the previously-untested SPA pages now reachable under `/app` — `AcceptInvitePage`, `safeRedirect`, and the Books/Audiobooks/Photo/Search/Music sub-pages plus the Settings Security tab (~15 new specs).
- chore(cleanup): remove the dead `src/api/music.ts` module (zero importers; `fetchArtists`/`fetchAlbumsByArtist`/`fetchTracksByAlbum` were superseded by the `ApiClient` music methods). Never bundled, so no `dist/` rebuild — internal source cleanup only.

tool_input.new_string:
## [Unreleased]

### Web UI
- **WS-D — owner/user surfaces migrated to the `/app` Vue SPA (Smarty retirement pending owner verification).**
  The hub's owner/user surfaces — **My Servers**, **Server Detail**, **Federation**, **Federation Shares**,
  **Shares**, **Shared With Me**, **Invite Links**, and **Requests** (plus the admin request-approval queue) —
  are now served by the shared `@phlix/ui` Vue SPA at `/app/*`, alongside the existing admin console at
  `/app/admin/*`. Route + nav registration lives in `web-ui/src/main.ts`. The equivalent Smarty SSR pages
  remain in place and are **not** removed — deletion is deferred until the new `/app` pages are verified live.
- **WS-D — public invite acceptance route `/app/invite/:token` (fixes the previously-broken `/invite/{token}` 404).**
  The public `GET /invite/{token}` link now redirects to the SPA `AcceptInvitePage` (`meta.public`, so an
  unauthenticated invitee reaches it without the auth guard). Not logged in → Log In / Sign Up buttons that
  carry a safe `?redirect=/app/invite/<token>` hop back here; logged in → an **Accept Invite** button calling
  `POST /api/v1/me/invite-links/{token}/redeem`, then a **View Shared Libraries** link to `/app/shared-with-me`.
  This restores parity with the retired Smarty `accept-invite` flow, whose PHP redirect had no matching Vue route.
- **WS-D — safe post-auth redirect.** `LoginForm`/`SignupForm` now honour a validated internal `?redirect`
  query (via the shared `@phlix/ui` `safeRedirect` guard, which only accepts same-origin `/app/` paths), so
  logging in / signing up from the invite page returns the user to accept the invite. Vitest specs cover all
  of the above pages.

### Tests

tool_input.old_string:
- **Web UI & admin console** — the hub's front door is a Vue SPA (the shared `@phlix/ui` design
  system) served at `/app` (`/` redirects to `/app/servers`): My Servers, Federation, and Shares for
  every signed-in user, plus a `requiresAdmin` **admin console** at `/app/admin/*` (Hub Dashboard,
  Users, Logs, Settings, Audit Logs). The original Smarty pages are deprecated and will be removed
  in a future release. Everything is backed by a full JSON API under `/api/v1` (incl. `/api/v1/admin/*`).
tool_input.new_string:
- **Web UI & admin console** — the hub's front door is a Vue SPA (the shared `@phlix/ui` design
  system) served at `/app` (`/` redirects to `/app/servers`). Every signed-in user gets **My Servers**,
  **Server Detail**, **Search**, **Federation**, **Federation Shares**, **Shares**, **Shared With Me**,
  **Invite Links**, and **Requests**; there is a public **invite-acceptance** page at `/app/invite/:token`
  (the target of the public `GET /invite/{token}` link). On top of that a `requiresAdmin` **admin console**
  at `/app/admin/*` (Hub Dashboard, Users, Logs, Settings, Audit Logs, request approval). The original
  Smarty pages remain in place while the migrated `/app` pages are verified, then are slated for removal.
  Everything is backed by a full JSON API under `/api/v1` (incl. `/api/v1/admin/*`).

- **Web UI & admin console** — the hub's front door is a Vue SPA (the shared `@phlix/ui` design
  system) served at `/app` (`/` redirects to `/app/servers`). Every signed-in user gets **My Servers**,
  **Server Detail**, **Search**, **Federation**, **Federation Shares**, **Shares**, **Shared With Me**,
  **Invite Links**, and **Requests**; there is a public **invite-acceptance** page at `/app/invite/:token`
  (the target of the public `GET /invite/{token}` link). On top of that a `requiresAdmin` **admin console**
  at `/app/admin/*` (Hub Dashboard, Users, Logs, Settings, Audit Logs, request approval). The original
  Smarty pages remain in place while the migrated `/app` pages are verified, then are slated for removal.
  Everything is backed by a full JSON API under `/api/v1` (incl. `/api/v1/admin/*`).

tool_input.old_string:
### Added

- **SV-3.3 — loudness normalization + client capability negotiation.**
tool_input.new_string:
### Added

- **WS-D — media SPA pages are now reachable under `/app` (Smarty retirement pending owner verification).**
  `web-ui/src/main.ts` now registers routes **and** top-bar nav entries for SPA pages that shipped in
  `@phlix/ui` but were previously unreachable on the server: **Books** (`/app/books`, `/app/books/:id`,
  `/app/books/:id/read`), **Audiobooks** (`/app/audiobooks`, `/app/audiobooks/:id`, `/app/audiobooks/:id/play`),
  **Photos** (`/app/photo/albums`, `/app/photo/album/:id`, `/app/photo/photo/:id`, `/app/photo/slideshow`),
  **Search** (`/app/search`), and the **Music** sub-pages (`/app/music/artists`, `/app/music/artist/:name`,
  `/app/music/album/:name`, `/app/music/tracks`, `/app/music/player`), plus nav links (Music, Books,
  Audiobooks, Photos, Search). The passkey/WebAuthn surface is now a **Security** tab on the SPA Settings
  page (`/app/settings/security`) rather than a standalone page. The equivalent Smarty SSR templates and
  their routes remain in place — deletion is deferred until the migrated `/app` pages are verified live.
- **SV-3.3 — loudness normalization + client capability negotiation.**

  otherwise). Behavior is unchanged for the real (string) rows; the fix clears the level-9 error surfaced
  when wiring up the newly-reachable `/app/music/tracks` page.

## [Unreleased] - 2026-07-18

### Fixed

- **WS-A — Continue-Watching now shows poster images and a progress bar in the `/app` SPA.**
  `PlaybackController::getContinueWatching()` (which backs both `GET /api/v1/me/continue-watching` and `GET /api/v1/users/me/continue-watching`) now runs every row through `MediaItemShaper::shape()`, so each entry gains the same top-level fields the SPA `MediaCard` renders: `id` (= **media item id**, so cards navigate to the correct detail page — previously the playback-state id), `poster_url`, `poster_srcset`, `runtime` (minutes), `year`, and `rating`. For **episodes** a two-level `LEFT JOIN` (season → series) resolves the **series** poster (falling back to the season poster, then the episode's own poster) *before* shaping, replacing the TMDB still/screengrab that previously showed. tool_input.old_string:
### Web Portal
- **Smarty-based Templates**: Server-side rendered HTML pages using Smarty
- **REST API Endpoints**: Complete API for library browsing, media info, and user data
- **JWT Authentication**: Integrated token-based auth with refresh support
- **Responsive Design**: CSS-first approach with utility classes
- **JavaScript Client**: ApiClient helper with auth, library, and player helpers
- **Continue Watching**: Track and display in-progress media
- **Library Browser**: Browse media by library with item counts
tool_input.new_string:
### Web Portal
- **Vue SPA (`/app`)**: The primary web UI is the shared `@phlix/ui` Vue SPA. As of **WS-D**, the media
  surfaces that shipped in `@phlix/ui` but were previously unreachable on the server are now registered
  (routes + nav) in `web-ui/src/main.ts`: **Books** (`/app/books`, `/app/books/:id`, `/app/books/:id/read`),
  **Audiobooks** (`/app/audiobooks`, `/app/audiobooks/:id`, `/app/audiobooks/:id/play`), **Photos**
  (`/app/photo/albums`, `/album/:id`, `/photo/:id`, `/slideshow`), **Search** (`/app/search`), and the
  **Music** sub-pages (`/app/music/artists`, `/artist/:name`, `/album/:name`, `/tracks`, `/player`), plus
  Music/Books/Audiobooks/Photos/Search nav links. Passkey/WebAuthn management is a **Security** tab on the
  SPA Settings page (`/app/settings/security`).
- **Smarty-based Templates**: Server-side rendered HTML pages using Smarty. These remain in place as the
  `/app` SPA pages above are verified live — Smarty retirement is deferred, not yet done.
- **REST API Endpoints**: Complete API for library browsing, media info, and user data
- **JWT Authentication**: Integrated token-based auth with refresh support
- **Responsive Design**: CSS-first approach with utility classes
- **JavaScript Client**: ApiClient helper with auth, library, and player helpers
- **Continue Watching**: Track and display in-progress media
- **Library Browser**: Browse media by library with item counts
